behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: source collectors -> aggregation/orchestration -> summary input payload. A unit test of a single merge function can miss wiring bugs where one source is not fetched, mapped, or forwarded. No real OS/network is required if source clients are in-process fakes. Higher-level rejection: Agentic is not required because this behavior checks input to generation, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real orchestration modules with three fake source adapters returning distinct sentinel fields. Execute one report build and assert the exact payload handed to the summary-generation interface contains all three source sections and expected key fields from each.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: The failure appears in cross-module error propagation and fallback rendering (source adapter failure -> orchestrator continuation -> report section note). In-process fakes can reproduce this by raising adapter errors, so System is not strictly required. Higher-level rejection: Agentic is disqualified by behavior text focusing on fallback note/continuation, not AI output correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Use real report orchestration with two fake sources returning data and one fake source raising an unavailable error. Assert process completes, report is generated, failed section contains `data unavailable`, and other sections remain populated.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on the model's actual summarization decisions (content fidelity), which a stubbed response cannot validate. This is the deterministic-assertion trap: wrapper tests can pass while real model output misstates values. Higher-level rejection: Workflow is disqualified because behavior describes one model generation step, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real summary-generation model on representative inputs with known key metrics/issues/deploy counts, then evaluate whether generated narrative preserves those key values and does not introduce contradictions/hallucinated numbers (with repeated runs and acceptance thresholds for variability).

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires end-to-end delivery to a real inbox, crossing real network/protocol boundaries (APIs + email transport). Mocks can hide SMTP/API auth, transport, formatting, or inbox-delivery failures. Higher-level rejection: Agentic is disqualified because pass/fail criterion is delivery to recipient inbox, not quality of model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the scheduled report job in an environment using reachable test instances of the three data sources and a real test mailbox. Assert the email arrives in the configured inbox with expected subject/body/report sections and no pipeline aborts.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is orchestration/state handoff across modules over two runs: generation -> delivery failure handling -> filesystem persistence -> later retry path. This can be caught with in-process wiring plus local filesystem and controlled double invocation. Integration-brake checks: no required real OS/network/subprocess behavior, no AI output dependence, no multi-agent chain. Higher-level rejection: System is disqualified because behavior can be validated without real email infrastructure by deterministic unreachable/then-recovering delivery adapter behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run job once with fake email adapter set to unreachable; assert report file is persisted locally with retry metadata. Run job again with adapter healthy; assert persisted report is retried/sent and retry artifact state is cleared or marked delivered.
